narrative.headline: 2018 Toyota Camry: power train leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Power train and braking concerns dominate owner complaints for this generation Camry, with throttle response and transmission behavior appearing across multiple component categories. Owners consistently describe a hesitation or lag when accelerating from low speeds, where the vehicle is slow to respond even under firm pedal input. Brake-related complaints include reported malfunctions and failures of brake assist systems, in some cases tied to crashes.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report the transmission jerks and acceleration is extremely sluggish when re-applying the accelerator from near-stop speeds. · 1 fire · 3 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Owners report brake malfunction and brake assist failing to engage, with some incidents resulting in crashes. · 1 fire · 21 crash · 22 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the check engine and traction warning lights illuminating at startup, accompanied by shaking when the accelerator pedal is pressed. · 4 crash

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: Owners report a noticeable delay of several seconds before the vehicle responds and builds speed after the accelerator is pressed from a stop. · 7 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 71 reports (10%), linked to 1 crash, with no fires or injuries reported. A smaller but recurring complaint area.
